[[Category:Aelaki grammar]]

The '''quirky jussive''' is a construction used to express a jussive for the [[First_person|1st]] or [[Second_person|2nd]] person in [[Aelaki]].

== Formation ==
It requires a [[Pseudopronouns|pseudopronoun]] that is marked in [[Third_person|3rd person]]. This limits it to only some of the few "canonical pseudopronouns" which have no independent usage as pronouns.

== Pseudopronoun forms ==
{| class="wikitable"
! Usage !! Pseudopronoun !! Notes
|-
| Inclusive || '''Thijish''' || child gender, exclusive
|-
| 1p singular || '''Gatharash''' || "my body"
|-
| 1p singular || '''Pathasash''' || "my name"
|-
| 2p singular || '''Gajasash''' || "your body"
|-
| 2p singular || '''Pajasash''' || "your name"
|-
| Exclusive plural 1p || '''Gathatharash''' || "my body" (plural)
|-
| Exclusive plural 1p || '''Pathathasash''' || "my name" (plural)
|-
| Plural 2p || '''Gajajasash''' || "your body" (plural)
|-
| Plural 2p || '''Pajajasash''' || "your name" (plural)
|-
| Inclusive plural 1p || '''Gætharash''' || "my body" (inclusive)
|-
| Inclusive plural 1p || '''Pæthasash''' || "my name" (inclusive)
|}

== Semantics ==
The quirky jussive implies a '''loss of agency''' on the part of the subject. For example:
* "You should stop using heroin"
* "I should stop overeating"

It is much more common in the first person and often indicates a perception of the task being '''difficult''', or just that one should perform the task. Since the proper 1st person [[Imperative|imperative]] is strictly a promise, the quirky jussive fills the gap for suggestions and obligations.

== See also ==
* [[Quirky_optative]]
* [[Imperative]]
* [[Pseudopronouns]]
* [[First_person_nouns]]
